claims.claim: Finite tie-aware Best-of-N expected selected utility is implemented and Monte Carlo validated.
A controlled diffusion-world toy shows selected imagined score rising while selected real utility can stagnate or drop.
A 3-member small conditional denoising ensemble trains and is evaluated on held-out toy conditions.
Mode-collapsed and hidden-mode generators expose selected-tail rank distortion and diversity diagnostics.
claims.evidence: controlled rows for mode_collapsed and figure3_tail_diagnostics.png

claims.claim: Selection budget N and denoising budget K are separated in a CPU-controlled grid.
Pilot-label calibrated lower-confidence selection closes most of the oracle gap in the controlled optimistic toy at budget 32.
Budget 128 pilot repair is a stronger controlled repair than the budget 32 practical setting.
Adaptive Best-of-N deployment emits one gate decision with an explicit reason code.
Residual conformal calibration reports held-out lower-bound diagnostics.
Pilot repair reduces selected-tail hallucination on held-out learned diffusion-world-model conditions.
Near-100% closure is possible in the controlled toy when hidden hazard features are supplied.
External robotics or benchmark validation is implemented.
claims.evidence: intentionally out of scope for v1

claims.claim: The project solves robot planning or validates on real robots.
claims.evidence: blocked by README, docs/claims.md, and docs/final_audit.md

claims.claim: Best-of-N always helps; more samples always hurt; calibration always fixes the issue; diffusion likelihood equals real utility.
Universal 100% Best-of-N repair is guaranteed without additional information.
claims.evidence: blocked by hidden-mode impossibility note in docs/theory.md

claims.claim: This is just a renamed WAM project.
